eligibility: Small and marginal landholder farmer families with cultivable land holding up to 2 hectares in their name.
benefits: Financial benefit of ₹6,000 per year in three equal installments of ₹2,000 every four months, transferred directly to bank accounts.

scheme_name: PM Fasal Bima Yojana (Crop Insurance Scheme)
eligibility: All farmers including sharecroppers and tenant farmers growing notified crops in notified areas.
benefits: Low premium rates for farmers (1.5% to 2% for food & oilseed crops, 5% for commercial/horticultural crops) with full insurance coverage against natural calamities.
deadline: July 31 for Kharif season, December 31 for Rabi season

scheme_name: Rythu Bandhu Scheme (Farmer's Investment Support Scheme)
benefits: Financial assistance of ₹10,000 per acre per year (₹5,000 per acre per season for Kharif and Rabi) for purchasing agricultural inputs.
deadline: Before commencement of each cropping season

eligibility: All categories of farmers. Priority is given to Small/Marginal, SC/ST, and Women farmers.
benefits: 40% to 50% subsidy for purchasing farm machinery like tractors, rotavators, power tillers, and harvesters.
deadline: Varies by state (Usually applications open in April-May)

scheme_name: PM Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (Per Drop More Crop - Drip Irrigation)
eligibility: All landholders, agricultural cooperatives, and members of water user associations.
benefits: Up to 55% subsidy for small and marginal farmers and 45% for other farmers to install drip and sprinkler irrigation systems.
deadline: Year-round applications accepted via state agriculture portals
